question: What did we conclude was causing duplicate Stripe charges for the same order?
turns: Some customers are being charged twice through our Stripe integration. We see two successful PaymentIntent confirmations tied to a single checkout flow for the same order.

Our frontend retries the POST /checkout API call if it doesn't get a 200 within 3 seconds. Each call creates a brand new Stripe PaymentIntent with a freshly generated idempotency key, so Stripe treats each request as distinct.

Root cause: the idempotency key was randomly generated per request (UUID v4) instead of being derived from the order. So retries produced new keys, new PaymentIntents, and new charges. Fix: derive the Stripe idempotency key deterministically from the order ID — e.g. 'pay-{orderId}' — so any retry for the same order reuses the same PaymentIntent and Stripe deduplicates the charge automatically.
